title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: An orphan boy discovers on his eleventh birthday that he is a wizard and is whisked away to a magical school where he begins to uncover the truth about his parents and a dark force that took them from him.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A comfort-loving hobbit is pulled from his quiet life into a sweeping quest across mountains and forests to help a band of dwarves reclaim treasure guarded by a fearsome dragon.

title: The Lord of the Rings
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A small hobbit is entrusted with a ring of immense power and must journey across a war-torn land to destroy it before a dark lord can use it to enslave the world.

title: A Game of Thrones
author: George R.R. Martin
description: Noble families scheme and battle for control of a fractured kingdom while an ancient threat gathers unnoticed beyond an immense wall of ice in the north.

title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
description: A legendary figure living under a false name recounts the story of his impoverished youth, his education in a school of magic, and the mysteries that shaped his infamous reputation.

title: Mistborn: The Final Empire
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: In a world blanketed by ash under the rule of an immortal tyrant, a street thief with a rare magical gift joins a crew plotting to overthrow the empire.

title: The Way of Kings
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: On a storm-battered world, a fallen surgeon, a broken king, and a reluctant soldier are drawn toward an ancient prophecy of returning god-like warriors.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: A young heir is thrust into a struggle for control of a desert planet that holds the galaxy's most valuable resource, and discovers a destiny tied to prophecy and power.

title: Foundation
author: Isaac Asimov
description: A mathematician predicts the fall of a galactic empire and establishes a foundation of knowledge meant to shorten the coming dark age, setting in motion a plan spanning centuries.

title: Ender's Game
author: Orson Scott Card
description: A gifted child is trained at a military academy in orbit to command humanity's fleet against an alien threat, unaware of how far his training will be pushed.

title: The Martian
author: Andy Weir
description: An astronaut is stranded alone on Mars after his crew presumes him dead and must use his engineering knowledge to survive until a rescue can reach him.

title: Neuromancer
author: William Gibson
description: A washed-up computer hacker is hired for one last job that pulls him into a dangerous conspiracy involving artificial intelligence and corporate power.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: In a future society engineered for stability and pleasure, a visitor from outside the system forces its citizens to confront what they have given up for comfort.

author: George Orwell
description: A low-ranking worker for a totalitarian government begins to question the Party's control over truth, history, and thought, risking everything for a fragment of freedom.

title: Fahrenheit 451
author: Ray Bradbury
description: In a society where books are outlawed and burned, a fireman tasked with destroying them begins to question everything he has been taught to believe.

title: The Handmaid's Tale
author: Margaret Atwood
description: In a totalitarian state built on the ruins of the United States, a woman forced into reproductive servitude searches for a way to reclaim her identity and freedom.

title: The Hunger Games
author: Suzanne Collins
description: A teenage girl volunteers to take her sister's place in a televised fight to the death, becoming an unlikely symbol of rebellion against an oppressive government.

title: Divergent
author: Veronica Roth
description: In a rigidly divided society, a girl discovers she does not fit into any single faction and must hide her nature while a conspiracy threatens to tear the city apart.

title: The Da Vinci Code
author: Dan Brown
description: A symbologist is pulled into a murder investigation that uncovers a centuries-old secret society and a conspiracy hidden within famous works of art.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
description: When a woman vanishes on her wedding anniversary, suspicion falls on her husband as shifting perspectives reveal a marriage built on deception.

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
author: Stieg Larsson
description: A disgraced journalist and a brilliant hacker team up to investigate a decades-old disappearance that exposes a family's dark secrets.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A psychotherapist becomes obsessed with treating a woman who shot her husband and then never spoke again, determined to uncover the truth behind her silence.

title: And Then There Were None
author: Agatha Christie
description: Ten strangers are lured to an isolated island where they are killed one by one, each death matching an old nursery rhyme with no way to identify the killer.

title: Murder on the Orient Express
author: Agatha Christie
description: A famous detective aboard a snowbound train must solve a murder committed in a locked compartment, with every passenger a suspect.

title: The Hound of the Baskervilles
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: A detective and his companion investigate a legendary curse said to haunt a wealthy family on the fog-covered moors of Devon.

title: Sherlock Holmes: A Study in Scarlet
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: A detective with unmatched powers of observation partners with a former army doctor to solve a baffling murder in the streets of London.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A sharp-witted young woman clashes with a proud and wealthy gentleman, and both must overcome pride and misunderstanding before they can find happiness.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Bronte
description: An orphaned governess falls in love with her brooding employer, only to discover a devastating secret hidden within his household.

title: Wuthering Heights
author: Emily Bronte
description: A passionate and destructive love story unfolds across two generations on the windswept moors, driven by obsession, revenge, and loss.

title: The Notebook
author: Nicholas Sparks
description: An elderly man reads a story of young love to a woman with memory loss, slowly revealing that the story is their own.

title: Me Before You
author: Jojo Moyes
description: A free-spirited young woman takes a job caring for a paralyzed former businessman and unexpectedly changes both of their outlooks on life.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A young girl in a small Southern town watches her father defend a Black man falsely accused of a crime, learning hard lessons about justice and prejudice.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A mysterious millionaire's obsession with a lost love unravels the glamour and moral emptiness of a wealthy community during the Jazz Age.

title: Moby Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: A sailor joins a whaling voyage led by a captain consumed by his obsessive hunt for the great white whale that once maimed him.

title: War and Peace
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: The lives of several aristocratic families intertwine against the backdrop of the Napoleonic Wars, exploring love, ambition, and the search for meaning.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A destitute former student commits a murder he believes is morally justified, and is slowly consumed by guilt as an investigator closes in.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A young shepherd travels from Spain to the Egyptian desert in search of a treasure, discovering along the way that the journey itself holds the greater reward.

title: Life of Pi
author: Yann Martel
description: A boy stranded on a lifeboat in the Pacific Ocean must survive alongside a Bengal tiger, testing the limits of faith, fear, and endurance.

title: The Kite Runner
author: Khaled Hosseini
description: A man haunted by a childhood betrayal in Afghanistan returns years later for a chance at redemption amid a country transformed by war.

title: The Book Thief
author: Markus Zusak
description: Narrated by Death, the story follows a young girl in Nazi Germany who steals books and shares them with others as the world around her collapses into war.

title: All the Light We Cannot See
author: Anthony Doerr
description: A blind French girl and a German soldier boy have their paths converge in occupied France during the chaos of the Second World War.

title: The Nightingale
author: Kristin Hannah
description: Two sisters in Nazi-occupied France choose vastly different paths of resistance and survival as war upends everything they know.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping account of how Homo sapiens rose from an unremarkable species to dominate the planet through shared myths, cooperation, and revolutions in thought.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
description: A woman raised in a strict survivalist family with no formal schooling recounts her journey to eventually earning a doctorate from Cambridge.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: A practical guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones through small, consistent changes that compound into remarkable results over time.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: A Nobel laureate explores the two systems that drive the way people think, revealing the biases and shortcuts behind everyday decisions.

title: The Shining
author: Stephen King
description: A family caretaking an isolated hotel for the winter is slowly consumed by the supernatural forces haunting its halls, with devastating consequences.

title: It
author: Stephen King
description: A group of childhood friends reunite as adults to confront a shape-shifting evil that has terrorized their town for generations.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
description: A young solicitor's visit to a remote castle unleashes an ancient vampire whose arrival in England threatens everyone he encounters.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: A scientist's experiment to create life results in a being that turns against him, raising questions about ambition, responsibility, and what it means to be human.
publisher: Lackington, Hughes, Harding, Mavor and Jones
